item.sengoku.seigun_upgrade_smithing_tem: Add diamond or hihi'irokane armor, weapon, or tool
Add broken hilt or armor piece
sengoku.advancements.ancient_city.descri: Tread his grounds if you must, but make not a sound.
sengoku.advancements.ancient_city.title: A Ruined Monastery
sengoku.advancements.fallen_clan.descrip: Visit the ruins of a clan that fell victim to war.
sengoku.advancements.fallen_clan.title: Fallen Clan
sengoku.advancements.forbidden_sands.des: Japan has no deserts. But after a great battle, this land became riddled with the rotting remains of the victims.
sengoku.advancements.forest_of_tears.des: As Izanami and the Shikome chased Izanagi out of Yomi, she wept across the entire realm, nuturing the trees you see here.
Bear the gifts from a Shinto Shrine.
sengoku.advancements.hunting_rats.descri: Kill a shinobi lurking in the shadows.
sengoku.advancements.hunting_rats.title: Hunting Rats
sengoku.advancements.kill_akugyo.descrip: An enormous merfolk of the deep with a beautiful face and a deadly temper; beware the waters they haunt.
sengoku.advancements.kill_akugyo.title: Yokai Hunter: Akugyo
sengoku.advancements.kill_all_yokai.desc: Kill one of every hostile Yokai.
sengoku.advancements.kill_bakotsu.descri: Unlike the screeching rotting horse heads, you probably didn't need to put this one down.
sengoku.advancements.kill_bakotsu.title: Yokai Hunter: Bakotsu
sengoku.advancements.kill_datsueba.descr: Datsueba are a type of female Oni that, along with the male counterpart Keneō, dwell around the Sanzu River in Yomi, punishing souls that pass through. During a funeral, a gold coin is placed in the hands of the deceased to ensure a safe toll in order to pass these creatures.
sengoku.advancements.kill_datsueba.title: Yokai Hunter: Datsueba
sengoku.advancements.kill_dorotabo.descr: Dorotabō are the revived bodies of farmers who have been corrupted or overworked in the muddy paddy fields. With their body and spirit still intact, maybe they can be cured.
sengoku.advancements.kill_dorotabo.title: Yokai Hunter: Dorotabo
sengoku.advancements.kill_funayurei.desc: Funayūrei are vengeful water spirits who are said to be the remnants of those who drowned at sea. Banned from a proper afterlife, they somehow found a place for themselves here in the dragon king’s palace.
sengoku.advancements.kill_gaki.descripti: Gaki are spirits which live in horrible torment and are afflicted with constant suffering. They look vaguely human, but they have distended, bulging bellies and tiny, inefficient mouths and throats.
sengoku.advancements.kill_gaki.title: Yokai Hunter: Gaki
sengoku.advancements.kill_gashadokuro.de: Built from the rotting bones of the fallen; a towering skeletal yokai that roams and hungers for the living.
sengoku.advancements.kill_goryo.descript: Vengeful spirits of wronged nobles and warriors returned to settle scores as onryō.
sengoku.advancements.kill_goryo.title: Yokai Hunter: Goryō
sengoku.advancements.kill_gozuki.descrip: Name translated to “Ox head demon”. These barbaric creatures are thought to be descendants of Gozu; one of the great gatekeepers of hell and torturer of wicked souls.
sengoku.advancements.kill_gozuki.title: Yokai Hunter: Gozuki
sengoku.advancements.kill_hitotsume_nyud: A massive one-eyed monk-like yokai that prowls roads and devours travelers who cross its path.
sengoku.advancements.kill_jorogumo.descr: Spiders in Japan are notorious for their ability to shape-shift. Some take the form of a beautiful mistress to lure in foolish young men.
sengoku.advancements.kill_jorogumo.title: Yokai Hunter: Jorōgumo
sengoku.advancements.kill_kaichigo.descr: Kaichigo is the trickster spirit of a shell box come to life. Similar to turtles they will retreat in their shell when attacked. They possess teleportation powers similar to the Tsuchinoko and can curse their attackers with brief levitation.
sengoku.advancements.kill_kaichigo.title: Yokai Hunter: Kaichigo
sengoku.advancements.kill_kamiike_hime.d: A guardian spirit of sacred pools: a fishlike yokai often seen as an omen of the sea god's favor.
sengoku.advancements.kill_kanashibari.de: Those who avoid frequent sleep are said to expect visits from the Kanashibari. Soaring creatures of the night that feed on the tired and sleepless. They are also often found in Yomi due to the absence of rest in that realm.
sengoku.advancements.kill_kappa.descript: Known for their aquatic bodies and depraved nature, kappa are devilish river demons who hunt and devour humans who enter their waters. Despite their strength, if the water dish inside their head is emptied, their energy will drain and will be unable to move.
sengoku.advancements.kill_kappa.title: Yokai Hunter: Kappa
sengoku.advancements.kill_katawaguruma.d: Katawaguruma, meaning ‘crippled wheel,’ are an odd yet horrifying yokai that roams the scalding lava of the Sanzu River. They can be categorized as the female equivalent of the Wa nyūdō, where instead of being an oversized head of a monk, they appear as tormented, naked women riding single, flaming, ox-cart wheels.
sengoku.advancements.kill_kaze_no_kami.d: Kaze no kami are evil wind spirits, usually depicted as formless or invisible in most Japanese folk-lore. But up here it looks like they can be spotted as they tread along the cold winds.
sengoku.advancements.kill_keneo.descript: Keneō are a type of male Oni that, along with the female counterpart Datsueba, dwell around the Sanzu River in Yomi, punishing souls that pass through. During a funeral, a gold coin is placed in the hands of the deceased to ensure a safe toll in order to pass these creatures.
sengoku.advancements.kill_keneo.title: Yokai Hunter: Keneo
sengoku.advancements.kill_kobayakawa_soh: Those of Clan Kobayakawa shall tolerate you no longer.
What appears to be a regular dormouse, the Kodama Nezumi is capable of inflating like a balloon and rupturing in response to a very angry mountain god. Stay clear of them at all costs.
sengoku.advancements.kill_kojin.descript: Meaning 'shark person', Kōjin are merfolk that live along the South China Sea that have ink-black scales and eyes that glow like jade.
sengoku.advancements.kill_kojin.title: Yokai Hunter: Kōjin
sengoku.advancements.kill_kuchisake_onna: The Slit-Mouthed Woman. A vicious onryō who will attempt to pursue anyone who turns their eye towards their severed jaws. Do not look at her at any cost and she will let you be.
sengoku.advancements.kill_maikubi.descri: Three heads, three warlords, three deaths left unfulfilled. Driven by nothing but pure unadulterated rage, the Maikubi are combined spirits of three samurai warriors who died by beheading the other in a violent quarrel. A fight that even after death, did not end.
sengoku.advancements.kill_maikubi.title: Yokai Hunter: Maikubi
sengoku.advancements.kill_ningyo.descrip: A strange mer-creature with the ability to bring both fortune and misfortune.
sengoku.advancements.kill_ningyo.title: Yokai Hunter: Ningyō
sengoku.advancements.kill_nuppeppo.descr: A disgusting walking piece of rotten meat, do not let it touch you.
sengoku.advancements.kill_nuppeppo.title: Yokai Hunter: Nuppeppō
sengoku.advancements.kill_nure_onago.des: Literally meaning 'wet girl'. Nure onago are swamp-like yokai that take the form of a drenched young woman covered in foliage such as vines and fungi.
sengoku.advancements.kill_obariyon.descr: Obariyon are vicious, small imp-like yokai who latch onto their target's back piggyback style and grow heavier with every step. Eventually, crushing the wearer. The Obariyon became a cultural curse in a famous Noh theater due to a scene where two ronin encountered an Obariyon mounting a chicken. It's said that anyone in the audience during this part of the play is immediately rendered mad and savage.
sengoku.advancements.kill_obariyon.title: Yokai Hunter: Obariyon
sengoku.advancements.kill_okubi.descript: Ōkubi are the giant severed heads of a weeping old woman that aimlessly soars the endless void of Yomi. They sneeze explosive rocks on sight, but some legends account a samurai who redirects her attacks with his sword.
sengoku.advancements.kill_okubi.title: Yokai Hunter: Okubi
sengoku.advancements.kill_omukade.descri: Omukade are giant centipedes that dwell in dark, damp places such as caves and abandoned mines and sometimes the mountains. It is said once a regular centipede reaches a certain age they become these fowl monsters.
sengoku.advancements.kill_omukade.title: Yokai Hunter: Ōmukade
sengoku.advancements.kill_oni.descriptio: Once a punished wicked soul burning in the depths of hell. These ogres walk the earth to bring destruction and cruelty to humanity just for raw enjoyment. However they wither when gazed upon the eyes of Amaterasu.
sengoku.advancements.kill_oni.title: Yokai Hunter: Oni
sengoku.advancements.kill_oni_brute.desc: Also known as Onryoki, the Oni Brute is among the larger as well as the leaders of the many oni you will encounter on your journey.
sengoku.advancements.kill_onikuma.descri: The bears in Japan are beastly, the ones who live long enough more so.
sengoku.advancements.kill_onikuma.title: Yokai Hunter: Onikuma
sengoku.advancements.kill_otoroishi.desc: Despite their appearance and aggression, Otoroishi were once sacred creatures who fed on wicked people and small animals like birds. Often found on top of tori gates as an act of guardianship.
sengoku.advancements.kill_ozato.descript: The blind beast falls. May the victims of this fallen city be at rest.
sengoku.advancements.kill_ozato.title: Yokai Hunter: Ōzatō
sengoku.advancements.kill_ryujin.descrip: The Dragon god Ryūjin has been bested. He humbly accepts you as a worthy guest of Ryūgū-jō. Lucky you!
sengoku.advancements.kill_ryujin.title: Yokai Hunter: Ryūjin
sengoku.advancements.kill_sagari.descrip: Good riddance to that horse and its bloodcurdling scream. Killing it was the merciful thing to do.
sengoku.advancements.kill_sagari.title: Yokai Hunter: Sagari
sengoku.advancements.kill_sarugami.descr: Sarugami are aggressive monkey spirits who are the remnants of an ancient monkey cult whose followers have degenerated into yokai after their religion crumbled. They can be found in the mountains where their violent tendencies can be veiled from the world.
sengoku.advancements.kill_sarugami.title: Yokai Hunter: Sarugami
sengoku.advancements.kill_satomi_sohei.d: Clan Satomi will now hunt you for the rest of your days.
sengoku.advancements.kill_shikome.descri: When the creator god Izanagi went down to Yomi to retrieve his dead wife, Izanami. He was disgusted to see the rot and decay that had consumed her, so he fled. Izanami in anger, sent out the Shikome; Ugly hag women of Yomi to pursue Izanagi for his treachery and very nearly succeeded until he blocked off the entrance with a large obsidian boulder.
sengoku.advancements.kill_shikome.title: Yokai Hunter: Shikome
sengoku.advancements.kill_shinegami.desc: Shinigami are the spirits that seek, perpetuate and consume death. Their presence in the overworld is not of their physical form but of every person and creature falling to the ground as a hollow shell.
sengoku.advancements.kill_skeleton.descr: The human skeleton from the grounds of a great battle, rendered to life once more in search of a true death. Maybe they're larger ones out there.
sengoku.advancements.kill_skeleton.title: Yokai Hunter: Skeleton
sengoku.advancements.kill_takeda_sohei.d: A bounty for your head has been placed by the lingering Takeda Clan.
sengoku.advancements.kill_tatarigami.des: A curse-incarnate, Tatarigami are vengeful god-like spirits that come in many different forms. Bringing immense calamity and destruction to all that roam.
sengoku.advancements.kill_tengu.descript: Often summoned by irresponsible monks. The Tengu are legendary creatures known for their mischievous behavior. They are skilled with the sword and are hard to hit.
sengoku.advancements.kill_tengu.title: Yokai Hunter: Tengu
sengoku.advancements.kill_teruto_uo.desc: Teruto uo are holy mermaids sent by Ryūjin to guide those lost at sea with their shining scales.
sengoku.advancements.kill_tsuchigumo.des: Also known as the purse web spider, Tsuchigumo are immense demonic ground spiders that adopt similar traits to the Jorogumo. Tales suggest they were once regular spiders that have been turned into yokai.
sengoku.advancements.kill_tsuchinoko.des: Tsuchinoko are short, stumpy snake-like yōkai. Who are surprisingly agile despite their awkward body shape.
sengoku.advancements.kill_tsurara_onna.d: The icicle that turned into a woman. A bi-product of male lonliness
sengoku.advancements.kill_tsurube_otoshi: Said to be giant dismembered heads of either humans, oni, or tengu. Ranging from sizes from the size of a regular human head to the size of boulders.
sengoku.advancements.kill_tsurubebi.desc: These spirits that are molded by the fires of Yomi appear as either small or very large orbs of fire. Some researchers say they once inhabited the trees of the overworld.
Found deep in the mountains or caves, these creatures have become real pests for their diet of human-blood. Kill them before they burrow within the stone.
sengoku.advancements.kill_ubagabi.descri: A disembodied head of an evil apparition who has been punished for their acts of thievery in life. The fire they breathe is their eternal home.
sengoku.advancements.kill_ubagabi.title: Yokai Hunter: Ubagabi
sengoku.advancements.kill_umi_nyobo.desc: Umi nyōbō are dangerous yōkai who live in the Ryūjin's domain as protectors clad in fine coral armor. They are often portrayed as wives of Umibōzu.
sengoku.advancements.kill_umibozu.descri: Fishermen have often reported hallucinations of massive dark figures when sailing over sunken ocean monuments and temples taken refuge by a false god...
sengoku.advancements.kill_umibozu.title: Yokai Hunter: Umibōzu
sengoku.advancements.kill_umiotoko.descr: Aquatic creatures of red scales and yellow eyes. Often portrayed as servants to a much more malevolent deity.
sengoku.advancements.kill_umiotoko.title: Yokai Hunter: Umiotoko
sengoku.advancements.kill_ushi_oni.descr: Name translated to “ox-demon”. These six-legged abominations have adopted bovine traits and can rarely be found in swamps. They have recently been known for being the cavalry of many bandit groups. Fear this creature.
sengoku.advancements.kill_ushi_oni.title: Yokai Hunter: Ushi-oni
sengoku.advancements.kill_wa_nyudo.descr: Wa nyūdō (meaning 'Wheel Priest') is a type of yokai that takes the form of a tortured, grotesque head of a monk attached to a single ox-cart wheel. Despite their diet being the souls of the wicked that reside in local tree saplings, they are too encumbered in their eternal suffering to notice you. Mereley rendering them harmless.
sengoku.advancements.kill_wa_nyudo.title: Yokai Hunter: Wa Nyūdō
sengoku.advancements.kill_waira.descript: A hideous creature. Few who have encountered this rare beast describe it as an earthly brown colored boar with a single claw on each limb. It is theorized that they are transformed Otoroshi due to their similar appearances.
sengoku.advancements.kill_waira.title: Yokai Hunter: Waira
sengoku.advancements.kill_yamauba.descri: Once human, the Yamauba are old hag witches from the mountains capable of wicked incantations. Usually found in isolated huts or deep within the mountains at night.
sengoku.advancements.kill_yamauba.title: Yokai Hunter: Yama-uba
sengoku.advancements.kill_yuki_onna.desc: The Woman of Snow, Yuki-Onna often prey on lost travelers making their way through heavy snow storms. Her beauty is overwhelming, but her skin and her heart are cold as ice.
sengoku.advancements.kill_yurei.descript: Yūrei are among the many types of spirits that are robbed of a peaceful afterlife, please avoid making eye contact.
sengoku.advancements.kill_yurei.title: Yokai Hunter: Yūrei
sengoku.advancements.occupied_castle.des: A castle that once belonged to a great Daimyo, now riddled with the armies of the Saika ikki mercenary clan.
sengoku.advancements.ride_bakotsu.descri: Bakotsu are the souls of horses that been subjected to a fiery death caused by the carelessness of their former owners.
sengoku.advancements.ride_bakotsu.title: Yokai Interaction: Bakotsu
sengoku.advancements.ride_sagari.descrip: Sagari are the remains of horse corpses that have been left to rot on the roads of Japan. Their whines and cries will strike those nearby with a terrible fever until the beast is perhaps comforted.
sengoku.advancements.ride_sagari.title: Yokai Interaction: Sagari
sengoku.advancements.the_black_hair.desc: Discovers the estate ruins of a regretful samurai lord.
sengoku.advancements.volcanic_soul.descr: Kagutsuchi, the son of Izanami that inevitably killed her. While his body is scattered in the Overworld, it appears Yomi has homed his soul down here to remind and symbolize Izanami’s regrets.
sengoku.advancements.volcanic_soul.title: Soul of a fire god.
sengoku.advancements.volcanic_wastes.des: Kagutsuchi was the kami of fire and destruction. With fires so hot, his mother Izanami was killed from his fierce heat during the process of his own birth. His father Izanagi was so distraught by the sacrifice made for his existence that he picked up his great sword and severed Kagutsuchi's head clean off. His remains are now scattered across Japan as volcanoes.
sengoku.advancements.yokai.root.title: Yokai Hunter
sengoku.advancements.yokai.root.descript: A list of every beast and demon of Japan
